title: ulmus × androssowii var. subhirsuta
intro: Ulmus × androssowii var. subhirsuta, also known as Siberian Elm, is a deciduous tree species that is a hybrid of Ulmus pumila and Ulmus minor. The bark of the tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Siberian Elm include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Siberian Elm can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its bark can also be used to make a medicinal paste or powder for topical application.
